tier: APFS directory/inode child-count (valence) correctness in the fs-tree record builder
host: Local WSL2 archlinux (qemu-system-x86_64 -enable-kvm, macOS Sequoia recovery, apfs_kext 2332.101.1) with a 64 MiB dir+child image passed through
bug: buildFsTreeRecords mis-counted directory children: the root inode's nchildren counted every file (including files nested in subdirectories), and every directory inode reported nchildren 0 regardless of its actual children. This is the foundation the full-tree COW commit (A2-3.2) builds on, and it also corrupts the legacy createRawRootDirectory / writeRawRootDirectoryFile production directory path.
fix: Added directChildCount(directoryId, files, extraSubdirectories) - counts files whose parentDirectoryId is that directory; the root additionally parents the top-level directories. buildFsTreeRecords now sets the root inode's nchildren to (root files + directories) and each directory inode's nchildren to its own children; appendRootDirectoryRecords takes the child count. A flat root (no directories, every file parented to root) reduces to files.size(), byte-identical to the certified single-level layout.
container: 64 MiB single-CIB image, volume DCTEST: root directory 'docs' containing child file 'a.txt' (18 bytes 'child-file-content'), built via the CLI create-image-root-directory + write-image-root-directory-file (which rewrite through buildFsTreeRecords)
linux_apfsck: apfsck -cw clean (only the pre-existing benign 'Data stream xfield: wrong flags.' note) both before and after the fix - apfsck does not perform the directory valence check, so it could not catch this bug.

apple_fsck_apfs.before_fix: fsck_apfs -n reported 'error: directory valence check: directory ... nchildren (2) does not match' and 'nchildren of inode ... does not match expected value (1)' - The container could not be verified completely. Evidence apfs-dir-child-counts-before-fix-fsck-errors.png.

after_fix.kernel_mount_read: the macOS kernel auto-mounted DCTEST read-write; ls showed /Volumes/DCTEST/docs and cat /Volumes/DCTEST/docs/a.txt returned 'child-file-content'
after_fix.command: fsck_apfs -n /dev/disk1 (unmounted after the kernel auto-mounted it read-write)
after_fix.container_verdict: The container /dev/disk1 appears to be OK.
after_fix.volume_verdict: The volume /dev/rdisk1s1 appears to be OK.
after_fix.evidence: apfs-dir-child-counts-fsck-clean.png (fsck verdict), apfs-dir-child-counts-kernel-read.png (kernel ls + cat of the directory child)

note: Caught only by the macOS kernel-fsck valence check that apfsck does not model. This is increment 1 of the full-tree COW commit (A2-3.2); the remaining work threads the full tree (root files + directories + children) through the COW commit so an in-place mutation preserves directories instead of failing closed.
